learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end integration landscape
learningObjectives.description: Explain SAP TM's role within integrated SAP and partner logistics ecosystems.

learningObjectives.title: Understand system responsibilities
learningObjectives.description: Describe which SAP component handles each logistics and financial capability.

learningObjectives.title: Understand enterprise business flow
learningObjectives.description: Clarify complete process from order creation to carrier payment across systems.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management is the central transportation execution application within the SAP Digital Supply Chain. It integrates with ERP, Warehouse Management, Finance, Business Network, GPS providers, carriers and cloud platforms to provide an end-to-end logistics solution from order creation to carrier payment.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: No SAP TM implementation works independently. Enterprise-wide integration is the foundation of every SAP TM solution.

A customer places a Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA. SAP TM receives the delivery, creates Freight Units and Freight Orders, SAP EWM manages warehouse loading, SAP Business Network sends electronic freight tenders to carriers, GPS devices provide shipment tracking, SAP TM performs Freight Settlement and SAP Finance completes the carrier payment. All systems exchange information automatically through SAP BTP Integration Suite.

sections.title: Core SAP TM vs Integrated SAP Landscape
Best Practice
sections.content: Adopt SAP standard integration architecture using SAP BTP Integration Suite and SAP Business Network instead of custom point-to-point interfaces. This improves scalability, maintainability and future upgrades.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational retail organization integrated SAP S/4HANA, SAP TM, SAP EWM, SAP Business Network, GPS providers and SAP Analytics Cloud. More than 100,000 shipments per month were managed through a unified digital logistics platform with complete end-to-end visibility.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: One of the most common SAP TM certification questions is identifying which SAP solution performs which responsibility. Memorize the integration landscape: S/4HANA → TM → EWM → Business Network → Finance.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not assume SAP TM performs every logistics activity. Transportation planning, warehouse execution, finance, analytics and collaboration are distributed across specialized SAP solutions.

items: SAP TM is the transportation hub within the SAP Digital Supply Chain.
SAP S/4HANA creates business transactions and master data.
SAP Business Network enables carrier collaboration.
SAP BTP provides cloud integration services.
SAP Finance processes accounting and carrier payments.
Together these systems deliver complete end-to-end logistics management.
